curmudgeon_rubric: DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 (design rubric used for EXP-211-redesign and EXP-249 et al.) plus content-specific checks listed in EXP-253.curmudgeon_review_focus.
integration_gate: Per HNOTE-OPERATOR-4-2-2-MISSTATEMENT-LEAD-001 explicit directive: route through curmudgeon for adversarial review BEFORE integration. Do NOT auto-integrate.

hnote_status_recommendation.note: HNOTE-OPERATOR-4-2-2-MISSTATEMENT-LEAD-001 is a single-deliverable HNOTE (one EXP, one section update). On decider integration of EXP-253, mark this HNOTE consumed (not on analyst delivery — per existing convention used by 18:20Z, 19:09Z, 20:01Z analyst runs and confirmed in CLAUDE.md). Set consumed_by="EXP-253", consumed_at=integration timestamp.

summary: EXP-253 rewrites the lead of section 4.2.2 to call out the dome's explicit misstatement of standard physics as predicting zero magnetic anomaly during eclipses (verbatim quotes from live.html Domain 11 panel + countdown banner, captured 2026-04-25). The existing Chapman 5-25 nT content is preserved as supporting prose. A new closing paragraph makes the BOU 2017 self-falsification observation explicit (the dome's own −10.9 nT baseline sits well outside its published ±3 nT-of-zero falsification window). Secondary patch: a single bridging paragraph at the top of 4.2.3 links the hedging structure to the misstatement (the hedging only LOOKS like hedging if the alternative is misstated as zero). TLDR rewritten to lead with the punchline. All HNOTE requirements addressed: live quote verification block included, scope finding documented (live.html-specific, broader prose framing flagged for curmudgeon to test/tighten), kernel-of-truth + counterargument blocks per user preference. Word count delta: ~+295 words for 4.2.2, ~+40 words for 4.2.3 prepend.
what_curmudgeon_should_check: Verify both dome quotes are still present on https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html at curmudgeon-review time. Cross-check raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 104 and 190. If any quote has been removed or reworded since 2026-04-25, capture the diff and adjust the prose framing.

Test scope: is the 'globe predicts 0' framing universal in the dome's eclipse material, or limited to live.html? Search raw-text/03-predictions.txt, raw-text/02-wins.txt, raw-text/05-model.txt, and any eclipse-specific subpage. If limited to live.html, suggest tightening 'the dome's eclipse argument depends on...' to 'the dome's live falsification dashboard depends on...'. Current draft uses the broader form because live.html IS the dome's public scoreboard, but narrower may be more defensible.

BOU 2017 self-falsification logic: confirm the dome currently uses BOU 2017 −10.9 nT as a baseline (raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 23: 'BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) | W004 empirical (-22.24 nT)'). If the dome has retreated to W004-only or replaced the baseline, the closing paragraph needs softening. Note WIN-010 caveat ('disturbed-day baseline') — does this give the dome an out? Draft argues no (cannot have it both ways) but stress-test.

Apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric: TLDR readability (≤3 sentences, plain language, punchline first — currently 3 sentences with punchline first), prose cadence, ps-detail formatting, no double-em-dash, em-class='term' used consistently with surrounding sections.

Stress-test the kernel-of-truth: is publishing a numerical falsification criterion really methodologically sound, or is the criterion construction (compare ECM to a strawman version of standard physics) so flawed that the act of publishing the number is itself misleading? Operator-friendly counterargument is the steel-man; curmudgeon should test whether the kernel survives that critique.
Check that the 4.2.3 prepend sentence reads naturally with the existing first paragraph. Alternative one-sentence inline form is documented in EXP-253.secondary_patch.notes if curmudgeon prefers.
Anchor preservation: confirm decider can locate <details id='p4-422'>...</details> as a single block in part4.html (no nested details with id collisions). Same for p4-423.

live_quote_verification_summary.method: curl https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html (HTTP 200, ~1181 lines) + cross-check against poller-archived raw-text/08-live-power.txt (mtime 2026-04-25T20:59:15Z). Both quotes present and byte-identical between live and archive.
live_quote_verification_summary.quote_1_verified: Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction). [live.html: Domain 11 falsification list <li> entry, byte-identical to raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 104]
live_quote_verification_summary.quote_2_verified: ECM predicts -[value] nT, globe predicts 0 nT. Requires Kp < 2. [live.html eclipse countdown banner JS innerHTML, byte-identical pattern to raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 190]

framing_summary.structural_argument: The dome's eclipse-as-discriminator argument requires three pieces: (1) ECM makes a specific prediction (yes — the FSF formula yields −5 to −26 nT across the 14 stations), (2) the globe makes a specific DIFFERENT prediction (claimed: 0 nT — false; actual: 5-25 nT per Chapman 1933 + 90 years of measurements), and (3) the eclipse data discriminates between them. Piece 2 is fabricated. The misstatement makes piece 3 look possible; the actual literature collapses pieces 1 and 2 into the same range.
framing_summary.self_falsification: BOU 2017 −10.9 nT is documented in dome's own raw-text as one of two baselines for the V51 eclipse formula. By the dome's own published criterion (±3 nT of 0 falsifies), −10.9 nT falsifies. The dome cannot consistently use BOU 2017 as input AND claim the globe predicts 0 nT — the same measurement is BOTH the dome's anchor AND a counter-example to the dome's falsification rule.
framing_summary.cross_section_links: 4.2.2 (this lead, misstatement) → 4.2.3 (hedging only looks like hedging because of the misstatement) → 4.2.5 (no dome geometry actually enters the formula) → 4.2.6 (commit to revisit if Chapman-window data outside expected range) → 4.2.7 (planned, EXP-254: parallel geometric trilemma). The reader can follow the structural argument across all five sub-sections in order.

next_phase_steps: Decider: queue EXP-253 for curmudgeon review (do NOT auto-integrate). On curmudgeon clear, integrate replacement_html into part4.html for p4-422, apply secondary_patch for p4-423, run node build.js html and tests/run.sh, commit with message referencing HNOTE-OPERATOR-4-2-2-MISSTATEMENT-LEAD-001 + EXP-253. Then mark HNOTE consumed.

Next analyst run (Mode 2): pick up HNOTE-OPERATOR-NEW-SECTION-4-2-7-ECLIPSE-GEOMETRY-001 → draft EXP-254 (new section 4.2.7 + 2 SVG illustrations). Per HNOTE ordering: 4.2.2 (this EXP) lands first; 4.2.7 lands after.
After 4.2.7 lands: revisit 4.2.6 to ensure forward-references to 4.2.7 are added (not in scope of EXP-253 or EXP-254 individually).

build_impact.test_suite: tests/run.sh — should pass without changes; no new numeric claims that affect data tests. Both quoted dome values (±3 nT, 0 nT) and Chapman range (5-25 nT) and BOU 2017 (−10.9 nT) are already present in the codebase.
build_impact.html_regen: node build.js html — single file regen, no asset changes, no nav changes.

newsworthy: Dome model's eclipse 'discriminator' depends on misstating standard physics as predicting zero — and by its own published falsification rule, it has already failed.
